frame_label: Glorification of Mother's Sacrifices / Mom's Home Cooking
generated_comment: Yaad hai, Ammi ke haath ka haleem? Woh taste aur khushboo ab kahin nahin milti. She used to make it with so much pyaar, subha se shaam tak. Aaj kal toh sab Maggi aur readymade mein hi khush hain. Par woh asli ghar ka swaad aur woh mehnat...uska toh koi comparison hi nahin.  #MaaKaKhana #HyderabadFood
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment glorifies the mother's extensive domestic labor (cooking haleem 'subha se shaam tak') by framing it primarily as an act of 'pyaar' (love), effectively erasing its recognition as work. It then implicitly sets this idealized standard by unfavorably comparing modern convenience foods ('Maggi aur readymade') to the mother's traditional efforts, implying a deficiency in contemporary women's domestic practices.
persona_text: You are Mohammed. Age 49. Married. A Muslim man in Hyderabad. Your daughter Sana who is 25 has been working at a private company for two years and is doing well. A proposal has come for her from a good family. The boy is 28, settled, working in the Gulf. His family has said that they expect the wife to be at home after marriage Ñ they are comfortable financially and do not need her income. Sana has said she does not want to stop working. You are sitting with your wife discussing what to say to Sana and whether this proposal Ñ which ticks every other box Ñ should be declined because of this one issue or whether Sana needs to understand what she would be giving up by holding onto this one condition.
